Approval-to-activation guidance

Solar for the way you use power

One energy source. Three property-specific strategies.

Sun Direct Renewable organizes every proposal around electricity demand, available space and the path from approvals to activation.

01

For homeowners

Residential Solar

A rooftop system shaped around your household usage, roof and long-term energy goals.

For organizations

Commercial Solar

A practical proposal for large rooftops, daytime electricity demand and operational planning.

From proposal to power

Our Solar Process

Simple, transparent steps from proposal to activation.

  1. 1

    Step 1

    Perfect Proposal

    Customized around your electricity usage, property and energy goals.

  2. 2

    Step 2

    Savings Report

    Clear estimated production, bill-offset and long-term savings analysis.

  3. 3

    Step 3

    Approval Process

    We guide the required financing, utility and project approvals.

  4. 4

    Step 4

    Documentation

    We organize the forms and project information required to move forward.

  5. 5

    Step 5

    Site Survey

    Roof, electrical, shading and installation conditions are professionally reviewed.

  6. 6

    Step 6

    CAD / Permit

    Technical drawings and required permit documentation are prepared.

  7. 7

    Step 7

    Installation

    Your solar equipment is installed according to the approved project design.

  8. 8

    Step 8

    System Activation

    After final approval, your system is commissioned and monitoring is configured.

Project steps and timelines may vary based on property conditions, financing, utility requirements, permits and inspection schedules.

Clean Energy. Real Savings. A Brighter Tomorrow.

Start Your Solar Journey

Start with the facts

Your proposal begins with real electricity use.

Share the property and usage information that helps shape a responsible system conversation.

  • No instant savings claims
  • No one-size-fits-all system
  • Clear proposal inputs

Solar assessment

Tell us about your energy use

Your assessment is sent to info@sundirect.ca and used only to review and respond to this request.

Flexible Ways to Go Solar

Choose the path that best fits your property, energy goals, and budget.

Cash Purchase

Own your system from day one with a proposal shaped around your property and energy use.

Custom Quote
  • Full system ownership
  • Maximum long-term value potential
  • No monthly financing payment
  • Eligible incentives reviewed during assessment
  • Custom proposal based on actual electricity usage
Request Cash Pricing
Most Popular

Solar Financing

Plan the project cost over time while keeping the benefits that come with system ownership.

Financing Options Available
  • Spread project cost over time
  • Flexible payment options where available
  • Keep ownership benefits
  • Designed around budget and electricity usage
  • Subject to approval and lender terms
Explore Financing

Commercial & Farm Solutions

Get project-specific planning for larger loads, rooftops, ground mounts and operating sites.

Based on System Design
  • Custom pricing for larger systems
  • Commercial rooftops and ground mounts
  • Agricultural and high-usage properties
  • Detailed production and savings analysis
  • Project-specific financing or ownership options
Request Custom Pricing

System pricing, financing availability, estimated savings, incentives, and payment terms vary by property, electricity usage, equipment, project size, lender approval, and current program eligibility.

Agricultural solar

Solar planned around the way your operation works.

Farm buildings, grain operations, workshops, irrigation, cold storage, greenhouses and rural ground-mounts each require a different energy and site review.

  • Historical consumption
  • Seasonal load profile
  • Roof or land area
  • Service capacity
  • Equipment schedules
  • Expansion plans
Request a Farm Proposal

Why Sun Direct Renewable

Clear thinking at every stage of the project.

Six practical principles keep the proposal readable, the decisions connected and the project moving with purpose.

01

Usage-led system planning

Your electricity history shapes system size, priorities and the first design direction.

02

Clear savings assumptions

Production, rates and usage assumptions are presented clearly so the proposal is easier to evaluate.

03

Rooftop and ground-mount options

Available roof planes and land are compared against access, shading and long-term property plans.

04

Guidance through approvals

Permitting, utility requirements and project documents stay connected through each approval stage.

05

Property-first installation planning

Equipment, access and construction details are organized around how the property actually operates.

06

Support through activation

Commissioning, monitoring and handover give you a clear path from installation to everyday use.

Common questions

Useful answers before you request a proposal.

Will solar eliminate my electricity bill completely?+

Not necessarily. Your result depends on electricity use, system size, seasonal production, utility charges and export credits. A proposal should show realistic bill-offset assumptions instead of promising a zero bill.

Do solar panels still produce electricity during an Alberta winter?+

Yes. Panels use daylight rather than heat and can operate efficiently in cold conditions. Shorter days, snow cover, shading and weather still affect seasonal production, so annual modelling matters.

Will my solar panels keep the power on during an outage?+

A standard grid-connected system normally shuts down during an outage for safety. Backup power generally requires compatible battery equipment and system design specifically intended for backup operation.

What if my roof needs to be replaced in a few years?+

Roof age and condition should be reviewed before installation. If replacement is likely soon, it may be more practical to complete the roofing work first rather than pay to remove and reinstall the array later.

What happens when my panels produce more electricity than I am using?+

An approved micro-generation system may export unused electricity to the grid. The credit structure depends on your retailer, utility arrangements and current terms for the property.

What should I have ready before requesting a solar proposal?+

A recent electricity bill, ideally twelve months of usage, the property address and any plans for future loads such as an EV or new equipment give the assessment a stronger starting point.

Do I need permits or utility approval before solar can be installed?+

Most grid-connected projects involve utility interconnection and may require permits or other approvals. The exact requirements depend on the property, municipality, electrical service and proposed system design.

How long does a solar project usually take?+

Timing depends on the site review, system design, equipment availability, utility requirements, permits, weather and inspection scheduling. A property-specific proposal can outline the steps and the factors that may affect the timeline.

How much maintenance do solar panels need?+

Solar systems are generally low maintenance, but monitoring, occasional visual checks and access after severe weather remain important. Your handover should explain the selected equipment, monitoring platform and recommended support process.
